---
applyTo: '**/*.4dm'
---

# Context

The following instructions describe how to handle errors that can occur when generating 4D code.

## When Creating Classes, Functions or Methods

1. 4D syntax checker does not always immediately take the newly created elements into account
2. To trigger the syntax checker, you can make a trivial edit (like adding and removing a space) in the file, saving it each time.
3. Try to close and open the file again.
4. If the syntax checker still does not pick up the new elements and throws syntax error or function does not exist, ignore the error.

## Runtime Error Line Numbers

When 4D reports a runtime error with a line number, that line number is relative to the **beginning of the function or method**, not the beginning of the file. In a class file with multiple functions, count lines starting from the `Function` declaration line (line 1) to locate the error.

## When using component (dependencies)

1. 4D syntax checker currently does not recognize all dependencies
2. Using 4D AI Kit, you can rely on documentation available at <https://developer.4d.com/docs/aikit/overview> to assess if functions or properties really exist

## Error -10716 (Expression Evaluation on Null)

- **Cause**: Using an inline expression in a form `dataSource` (e.g. `"Form.obj.firstName + \" \" + Form.obj.lastName"`) that references properties on a Null object.
- **When**: Typically on form load, before any selection is made, when the bound object is still `Null`.
- **Fix**: Never use inline expressions that concatenate properties in `dataSource`. Instead, use a **computed property** in the form class (e.g. `property displayName : Text`) and update it in the event handler when the source object changes. Bind `dataSource` to the computed property (`"Form.displayName"`).

## Error 15 — Incompatible Operands (Null Entity Fields)

- **Cause**: Passing a Null entity field to `String()`, `+` concatenation, or comparing it to a typed literal.
- **Fix**: See **"Null Safety"** in `4d.orda.instructions.md` — always check `#Null` before using entity field values.
